frameworks.name: neural manifold representation
frameworks.one_line: transitive relations encoded as geometry on a low-dimensional neural manifold

frameworks.name: few-shot knowledge assembly
frameworks.one_line: rapid reorganisation of relational codes triggered by minimal linking information

frameworks.name: stochastic online gradient descent
frameworks.one_line: SGD adapted to enable rapid relational reorganisation in a connectionist network

frameworks.name: deep neural network model of cognition
frameworks.one_line: network simulation matched to human flexible relational learning

core_question: How can neural codes for transitive relations be rapidly reorganised when new linking information reveals previously hidden structure?
key_assumptions: transitive orderings can be learned within distinct contexts before being linked
relational structure is encoded geometrically across dorsal frontoparietal cortex
few-shot reorganisation is feasible for both brains and gradient-based networks
manipulations: two distinct learning contexts each containing a transitive ordering of novel objects
exposure to linking information that bridges the two contexts
comparison of human BOLD responses with a neural network model
key_findings: BOLD signals in dorsal frontoparietal cortex showed rapid rearrangement of object representations after linking exposure
neural manifold reorganisation occurred after minimal linking information
an adapted SGD network model exhibits analogous rapid knowledge assembly
interpretations: flexible human cognition relies on few-shot reorganisation of relational neural codes
standard gradient-based learning can be modified to capture rapid knowledge assembly
